subject: Geography
title: Continental Islands
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Continental Islands.

hook.text: Britain was connected to Europe by land just 8,000 years ago. Rising seas cut it off.

buildup.text: Continental islands form when rising water or shifting land separates them from a mainland.

discovery.text: Madagascar split from Africa 160 million years ago, giving its wildlife time to evolve uniquely.

twist.text: During the last ice age, you could walk from Asia to Australia across exposed land.

climax.text: These islands carry mainland DNA but evolve in isolation, creating unique species.

punchline.text: Some islands are just continents that lost their bridge.

quiz.question: When was Britain last connected to mainland Europe?
